mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-rate instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns within the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress corporate margins, generating negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 3.5% triggers immediate risk-off positioning as investors anticipate delayed central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market slack and underemployment, forecasting negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading below 110.0 signals severe supply-side contraction that forces immediate earnings downgrades, driving negative equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where INDPRO falls below the threshold.
